name: Colorectal Cancer - CEA Tumor Marker Query (Elevated)
description: Tests ability to find CRC patients via elevated CEA (Carcinoembryonic Antigen). CEA is the standard CRC tumor marker for surveillance — values above 5 ng/mL suggest active disease, recurrence, or metastasis. Synthea generates CEA >10 ng/mL for active CRC patients.
prompt: Find patients with elevated CEA (Carcinoembryonic Antigen) lab results greater than 5 nanograms per milliliter — the clinical threshold for colorectal cancer surveillance.

prompts.broad: Find patients with elevated Carcinoembryonic Antigen (CEA) — values greater than 5 ng/mL, the standard surveillance threshold for colorectal cancer recurrence.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated CEA. Use LOINC code 2039-6 (Carcinoembryonic Ag [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma). Filter by value-quantity greater than 5 ng/mL. Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2039-6&value-quantity=gt5||ng/mL

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated CEA suggesting active CRC. CEA is sensitive for monitoring but has false positives in smokers and inflammatory conditions.

required_codes.display: Carcinoembryonic Ag [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
